title: Build platform to launch 24/7 trading agents from a prompt
author: mak55533
selftext:
I built my own long-running agent harness based on Claude Code SDK - always on agent that strictly follows given strategy, while being able to reason, can extract specific info from X, Reddit, Substack, Telegram, and other sources.

Some results:

Prompted to track r/ wallstreetbets: it found signals to buy ZM, and USO stocks: +9% total portfolio over 10 days

Prompted to read earnings calls, and apply specific heuristic rules: made +8% off TLS stock in 5 days.

Sent a collection of semiconductor analysis substacks and told to constantly read them: +9% this week

comments.author: Otherwise_Wave9374
comments.body:
This is pretty cool. The always-on harness is the part most people miss, the agent is only as good as the scaffolding (memory, tools, guardrails, and how you stop it from drifting).

How are you handling risk controls, like max position sizing and "do nothing" conditions when the signal is weak? Also curious if you do any paper trading / shadow mode before letting it touch real capital.

If you are thinking about productizing, https://www.agentixlabs.com/ has some good patterns for long-running agents (heartbeat checks, tool timeouts, human-in-the-loop triggers) that might fit a 24/7 trading setup.

replies.author: mak55533
replies.body:
Thanks, agree about always-on being the biggest benefit of agentic systems.  
Risk controls can be specified by users - such as max draw-down in a day, or max position size, and the agent will follow it vigorously. Agent also can decide not to pursue any trades if the signal is weak - and that is the case for some agents running.  
All the trades are running with simulation money, I receive real time updates about agents actions, and can place real trades myself.

replies.author: UnitedAcanthaceae118
replies.body:
Sounds similar to [Everstrike](https://everstrike.io), except that trading is manual.

How often does the agent make decisions? How many tokens are consumed in each decision?

replies.author: mak55533
replies.body:
Everstrike seems to be for crypto only.  
Agents set their own trading loop intervals dynamically - if there is an actively developing situation with high market reaction, and for example it actively reads twitter - it will set shorter loop, if the agent is analyzing some newsletter, it will set longer loop

comments.author: epicskyes
comments.body: Are you eventually going to add metrics to the site? Not specific names but at least something showing its activity?

replies.author: mak55533
replies.body: What metrics would you like to see? There is total portfolio value over time right now

comments.author: celzo1776
comments.body: Is there a git for the project?
